We raise our voices to match their transactional chatter, lifting the rising, stubborn volume of our morning prayers like a defensive wall, our voices binding together in a dense, melodic chorus to utterly drown out the dry rustle of their ledger sheets and the sterile hum of their tablets.

choice.text: Refuse to hear their proposals during the sacred hour.

prose: We let their metallic voices fill the chamber, yielding the physical air of the shrine to their dry rasp in exchange for a dedicated, high-frequency bandwidth reserved exclusively for the ancestral liturgy, allowing our traditional chants to ride their copper wires.

choice.text: Incorporate their signals into the station's sonic fabric.

thread.crossing: The low, continuous hum of the coolant lines vibrates through the thin soles of our silk slippers as {ireland_full_name} slides a heavy, cold-rolled steel ledger across the deck plates, its scraping weight dulling the digital pinging from the ceiling. This silent offering—a shared monopoly over the station's deep-space communications array—requires {surname} to sign away our ancestral claim to the dry-dock berths in exchange for their syndicate’s absolute loyalty during the upcoming corporate secession. One stroke of the stylus will bind our vast reach to their network of orbital runners, securing a modern dynasty at the cost of the old shipping lanes.

prose: Through the triple-vulcanized acrylic of the high observation gallery, the stars present no friendly shimmer; they are naked, needle-sharp punctures in a shroud of absolute charcoal, burning with the sterile, pitiless glare of unshielded fusion. Below, the virgin planet hangs in the blackness like a cold, bruised plum of iron and basalt, its vast plains of unwritten silt untouched by the terraced contours of the home provinces. {full_name} peers through the reflections on the thick pane, searching the curve of this alien sphere for even a phantom trace of the old world—the soot-blackened family shrines of the southern valleys, the red-paper lintels of the delta ports, or the yellow earth where five generations of ancestors dissolved into the silt of the Pearl River. There is only the thin, luminescent thread of the exosphere, a fragile blue membrane waiting to be sheared by the descending keels of their landing craft.

Behind {full_name}, the green phosphorus glow of the holographic charts casts long, sickly shadows across the faces of the migration committee, turning their features into the pale masks of ancestors carved from jade. These men, elders of the merchant guilds and representatives of the five thousand cryo-stowed souls slumbering in the hull, debate the social geometry of the new settlement like mandarins partitioning a conquered salt marsh. The {family_name} name carries the weight of a century of exile—from the early labor contracts on the western railways of old America to this final leap across the stars—and these people look to them for the thread of continuity that prevents a diaspora from becoming an oblivion. Before the chemical retro-rockets fire their final burns, the choice must be carved into the stone of this new sky: will they bind this wilderness to the old liturgies, open a secular valley of trade where every tong and company might stake its claim, or crown their own bloodline as the sole arbiters of the new soil?

The raw, unfiltered glare of the system's primary star strikes the heavy brass plate of the desk, throwing the sharp shadow of the signing-stylus across the crisp synthetic vellum of the colony's founding charter like a sundial marking the final hour of their old world.

choice.text: Step forward into the glare to seal the colony's civil destiny with your mark.

prose: You turn your back on the vacant, light-devouring horizon, finding instead the tiny, tallow-scented spark of the family oil lamp flickering inside its brass gimbal—a fragile, amber coal carried across light-years of silence to keep the domestic dead warm.

choice.text: Keep your gaze fixed upon the old domestic gods, guarding the sacred lineage against the void.

decision.prompt: With the chemical thrusters of the landing craft humming through the deckplates, how will the {family_name} clan lay the foundation of its new dominion?

options.text: Proclaim a Holy Covenant: establish a Theocratic Council to rule the colony through ancestral rites and sacred moral law.
Charter a Secular Trust: position the family as neutral arbiters and landlords of the primary valleys, charging tribute for the soil.
Build a Monastic Sanctuary: withdraw from the shifting tides of colonial politics to erect a mountain retreat for the uncorrupted rites.

prose: The alkaline bite of the first infusion on this red world is a cold, chemical betrayal—a flat, sulfurous rasp that tastes of thrice-reclaimed greywater and the dead, un-aerated iron of Martian silicate. It lacks the soft, limestone-kissed sweetness of the mountain springs that sustained their ancestors through the hard railroad winters of the old territory, yet as {given_name} holds the liquid against the roof of their mouth, there is a fierce, concentrated heat burning behind the mineral sting. In the cramped, hum-riddled curve of the habitat's pressurized greenhouse, a solitary cutting of domestic plum-blossom struggles against its bed of synthetic loam, its leaves pale and chlorotic beneath the unblinking glare of the sodium-growth arrays. The family sits in a quiet circle upon crates of ribbed, industrial polymer, passing the single, chipped celadon cup like an illicit relic, each tongue measuring the price of their exile.

This is the inheritance poured into the small, dry mouths of the children who crawl upon the anti-static mats of the bay floor: a sharp, metallic tincture of endurance. The old blue cradle of their ancestors has shrunk to a cold, nameless spark in the overhead viewfinders, but the ancestral rites have not dissolved in the hard vacuum of the transit. When the cup reaches the youngest heir, the boy swallows the dregs with a silent, tightening jaw, the sour, iron-tinged fluid stinging the back of his throat like a dry-land sacrament—a bitter oath that wherever the blood of the {family_name} is split or settled, the dry silt of these nameless spheres will be forced to yield to their altars.

We spill the lukewarm, bitter dregs directly onto the gray, sterile silt beneath the metal growth-tray, watching the dry dust hiss and swallow the brown liquor, sealing our blood-compact with this cold, unwatered crust.

choice.text: Consecrate the new planet as our permanent home.

prose: We pluck the sodden, shriveled leaves from the cup to dry against the thrumming exhaust vents, packing them into the lead-lined storage of the outbound probe to seed the next cold reach of the void.

choice.text: Declare that our pilgrimage is never truly finished.

decision.prompt: The bitter iron of this first harvest clings to your tongue, a harsh reminder that the old ways cannot survive on memory alone. How will {given_name} anchor the sacred rites of the {family_name} line in this lifeless dust?

options.text: We will bind our path to the colony's chief agriculturalist, sharing the sparse water of our quarters to raise children who will learn to coax both grain and grace from this iron sand.

We have no need for a spouse in these narrow domes; we shall instead adopt the temple's most devout acolyte to inherit the {surname} name, passing the litanies of the sweet water to a mind already purified by faith.

Let the physical line of {family_name} end with our breath. We will pour our remaining days into grafting the ancestral plum-blossom onto the Martian scrub, leaving a living temple of wood rather than children of clay.
